title: Eliminate ClaudeMessageContext type duplication, forward all MessageContext fields
analysis:
The codebase has two structurally identical interfaces: `ClaudeMessageContext` (bridge.ts:119-125) and `MessageContext` (claudeStore.ts:42-48), both with the same 5 optional fields. A compile-time `Equals<A,B>` assertion in types.typecheck.ts already enforces structural sync. The bridge function `claudeSendMessage` correctly maps all 5 fields camelCase→snake_case, and the Rust `MessageContext` struct in claude_bridge.rs accepts all 5. However, App.tsx:64-69 only destructures and forwards 3 of 5 fields (`selectedEntity`, `diagnostics`, `constraints`), silently dropping `currentFile` and `attachedContexts`.

The fix has two parts:
1. **Eliminate type duplication**: Replace standalone `ClaudeMessageContext` interface with a re-export of `MessageContext` from claudeStore.ts. This makes bridge.ts depend on the store type, but since bridge.ts is already a wire-layer utility and the type is a pure data interface (no store logic), the coupling is acceptable. The `types.typecheck.ts` assertion becomes unnecessary for this pair and can be simplified.
2. **Fix field forwarding in App.tsx**: Pass the entire `context` object to `claudeSendMessage` instead of cherry-picking 3 fields. Since both types are structurally identical (and will become the same type), this is safe.

The Rust sidecar already handles all 5 fields with `Option<>` + `skip_serializing_if`, so no backend changes needed.

steps.description: Write failing test in App.test.tsx: verify that `claudeSendMessage` receives `currentFile` and `attachedContexts` when the onSend callback is invoked with a full MessageContext. This test will fail because App.tsx currently drops those two fields.

Fix App.tsx onSend handler: replace the 3-field cherry-pick `{ selectedEntity: context.selectedEntity, diagnostics: context.diagnostics, constraints: context.constraints }` with passing `context` directly to `claudeSendMessage(text, context)`. This forwards all 5 fields.

Add compile-time test in claudeBridge.test.ts: import both `ClaudeMessageContext` and `MessageContext`, add a type-level assertion that `ClaudeMessageContext` is exactly `MessageContext`. This will pass now (types are identical) and will catch future divergence after step-4 removes the duplicate.

Eliminate ClaudeMessageContext duplication in bridge.ts: remove the standalone interface definition (lines 113-125). Import `MessageContext` from `./stores/claudeStore` and re-export it as `ClaudeMessageContext` via `export type { MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext }`. Update the JSDoc comment to explain this is a re-export for backward compatibility.

Simplify types.typecheck.ts: the `Equals<ClaudeMessageContext, _ExpectedClaudeContext>` assertion is now trivially true since ClaudeMessageContext IS MessageContext. Replace the Pick+Equals assertion with a simpler comment noting that ClaudeMessageContext is a re-export of MessageContext, and remove the now-redundant structural sync guard (lines 116-141). Keep the Equals utility and other assertions in the file intact.

Run full test suite (`vitest run` in gui/) to confirm all existing tests pass — including claudeBridge.test.ts (snake_case mapping for all 5 fields), types.typecheck.ts (compile-time checks), and the new App.test.tsx test (full context forwarding). Verify no TypeScript compilation errors.

design_decisions.decision: Re-export MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext rather than using Pick<>
design_decisions.rationale: Since both types are structurally identical (all 5 fields), there's no reason to use Pick — the types are the same. A re-export (`export type { MessageContext as ClaudeMessageContext }`) preserves the existing export name so all downstream imports (tests, etc.) continue working without changes, while establishing a single source of truth.

design_decisions.decision: Pass context object directly instead of spreading individual fields
design_decisions.rationale: In App.tsx, `claudeSendMessage(text, context)` is cleaner than `claudeSendMessage(text, { ...context })` and both achieve full forwarding. Since the types are now unified, TypeScript ensures type compatibility. Direct pass also prevents future field-dropping bugs when new fields are added to MessageContext.

design_decisions.decision: Keep ClaudeMessageContext as a named re-export rather than removing it entirely
design_decisions.rationale: Removing the export would require updating all import sites (tests, any other consumers). A type alias re-export maintains backward compatibility while eliminating duplication. The name also semantically signals 'this is the context shape used at the bridge layer'.

design_decisions.decision: Simplify rather than remove the types.typecheck.ts assertion
design_decisions.rationale: The assertion becomes trivially true once ClaudeMessageContext is a re-export of MessageContext. We simplify to a comment rather than keeping dead assertions, but preserve the Equals utility type since it's used by other assertions in the same file.

reuse.how: Import and re-export as ClaudeMessageContext in bridge.ts, eliminating the duplicate definition
Follow the same mockInvoke pattern for any new bridge-level assertions
Follow existing Tauri mock patterns (vi.mock @tauri-apps/api/core) for the new onSend forwarding test
Already defined in the file; reuse for any new compile-time type assertions if needed
